sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Malvern-Based Annovis Continues Phase 3 Alzheimer’s Trial After Safety Review
feed.summary: Annovis Bio Inc. announced that its Phase 3 clinical trial for Alzheimer's disease with buntanetap will continue without modification following a six-month safety review by an independent Data and Safety Monitoring Board. The DSMB found no safety concerns, and the company anticipates a first symptomatic efficacy readout in early 2027 and a disease-modifying readout in early 2028. This recommendation supports the clinical program, with the FDA potentially considering combined safety data from both Alzheimer's and Parkinson's studies for a future new drug application.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Maintains Equal Weight on Phibro Animal Health Corporation (PAHC), Cites Margin Expansion
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has maintained an "Equal Weight" rating on Phibro Animal Health Corporation (PAHC) and increased its price target to $49 from $45, citing strong operational performance, margin expansion, and a better fiscal 2026 forecast. The upgrade followed Phibro's fiscal second-quarter results showing a 21% rise in net sales, a 41% increase in Adjusted EBITDA, and a 58% rise in adjusted diluted EPS. Phibro has also updated its full-year net sales estimates to $1.45 billion to $1.50 billion and adjusted EBITDA to $245 million to $255 million.

feed.title: Court dismisses NetApp complaint against ex-CTO now at VAST, but NetApp is appealing
feed.summary: A Florida court has dismissed NetApp's complaint against its former CTO and SVP, Jonsi Stefánsson, who now works at VAST Data. The court cited jurisdictional issues, determining that Icelandic courts held jurisdiction based on Stefánsson's employment contract and PIIA. NetApp has appealed this decision and is pursuing separate legal actions in Iceland.

feed.title: Portland General Electric to acquire renewable assets from PacifiCorp
feed.summary: Portland General Electric (PGE) is set to acquire select generation, transmission, and electric utility operations from PacifiCorp in Washington state for $1.9 billion, including various renewable energy assets. The acquisition involves the construction of two new solar and battery hybrid projects totaling 615 MW and the purchase of existing wind facilities. This move will significantly expand PGE's renewable energy portfolio and transmission infrastructure, with regulatory approval expected within 12 months.

feed.title: UBS Cite Efficient Capital Allocation and Rising Profits in Viatris Inc. (VTRS) Upgrade
feed.summary: UBS has upgraded Viatris Inc. (VTRS) to Buy from Neutral, raising its price target to $18, citing stronger growth prospects, a new product pipeline, continuous cost-cutting, rising profits, and efficient capital allocation. The company recently launched Inpefa in the UAE, the first market outside the U.S., with additional global launches planned. Viatris is expected to discuss its global rollout strategy during its Q4 and full-year 2025 results release on February 26, 2026, ahead of an Investor Event on March 19, 2026.

feed.title: Tractor Supply grand opening is Saturday; It’s more than a farm store
feed.summary: Tractor Supply has opened a new store in Pea Ridge, aiming to reduce travel for residents who previously had to visit stores in Bentonville or Cassville, Mo. The store's grand opening is scheduled for Saturday, Feb. 21, though it had a soft opening on Feb. 14. Mayor Nathan See highlighted the economic benefits, including increased tax revenue and job creation, emphasizing that the Pea Ridge location offers a wide range of products beyond traditional farm supplies, such as clothing, pet supplies, tools, and home goods.

feed.title: Antero Midstream Corporation (NYSE:AM) Short Interest Update

The company missed recent quarterly EPS expectations but revenue slightly topped estimates, and it announced a quarterly dividend of $0.225, though its high payout ratio raises questions about sustainability.

feed.title: Absci to Participate in Upcoming Investor Conferences
feed.summary: Absci Corporation (Nasdaq: ABSI), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company utilizing generative AI for therapeutic development, announced its participation in four upcoming investor conferences in March 2026. The company will engage in fireside chats and host one-on-one meetings with investors at events including the TD Cowen 46th Annual Health Care Conference and Leerink Partners 2026 Global Healthcare Conference. Webcasts of the fireside chats will be available on Absci's investor relations website.

feed.title: Hormel Foods' Preliminary Q1 Adjusted Earnings Projections Beat Estimates; Sells Minnesota Turkey Assets to Life-Science Innovations
feed.summary: Hormel Foods announced preliminary adjusted earnings projections for Q1 2026 that exceeded analyst estimates. Concurrently, the company has entered a definitive agreement to sell its Minnesota turkey assets, which include its whole-bird turkey business, to Life-Science Innovations. This strategic move aligns with Hormel's portfolio management and financial performance expectations.

feed.title: Tapestry and Climeworks Announce Carbon Removal Partnership To Advance Innovative Climate Action
feed.summary: Tapestry, Inc. (NYSE: TPR) has partnered with Climeworks, a leader in carbon removal solutions, through a 10-year agreement to acquire a diversified carbon removal portfolio. This collaboration marks Tapestry's first purchase of carbon removals, aiming to address its Scope 1 emissions and support innovation in climate solutions. Climeworks' portfolio includes engineered and nature-based solutions, making Tapestry its first North American fashion industry customer to engage in long-term, high-quality carbon removal.

feed.title: Phillips 66 Dividend Increase And New California Fuel Supply Approach
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) has increased its quarterly dividend and is reshaping its California fuel supply strategy after closing its Los Angeles refinery. The company will now lease storage tanks in the Bahamas and import gasoline into California, shifting from local production to a logistics and trading-based model for that market. This move signals management's confidence in financial performance and cash generation, but also introduces new logistical risks and potential impacts on margins.

feed.title: Alnylam’s First Profitable Year Tests Durability Of TTR RNAi Franchise
feed.summary: Alnylam Pharmaceuticals (NasdaqGS:ALNY) has achieved its first full year of profitability in 2025, driven by the success of its TTR franchise and expanded Amvuttra indication. The company is also investing in a new RNAi manufacturing platform and forming new industry partnerships to broaden its RNA therapeutics portfolio. Key challenges include reliance on its TTR franchise and effective capital allocation for R&D and manufacturing to sustain its growth.

feed.title: Amgen Stock Is Up 14% This Year on Dual Drug Approvals. Can the Rally Hold?
feed.summary: Amgen (AMGN) stock has risen 14% year-to-date, driven by recent FDA and European Commission approvals for its drug UPLIZNA. Despite strong revenue and EPS beats, the company missed significantly on free cash flow in Q4 2025. The article discusses analyst targets, market sentiment, and valuation models, suggesting potential upside if pipeline execution and margin recovery continue, with key catalysts including the Citi Oncology Leadership Summit and initiation of Maritide Phase 3 trials.
